notes: v1.14.131 - STAGE 5: demote legacy chrome to the ?legacy=1 rip-cord only (hide, not delete - fully reversible). Audit of the default (body.rd) UI confirmed the redesign frame already demotes most legacy chrome: the 5-tab nav, app-logo/loc, mode-toggle, and search bar are body.rd-hidden, and the NOW dashboard / Today / Mission Brief all live on pg-triage which is never the redesign home (pg-cmd is) and is never active in the default UI (Stage 2 gated the bounces). The ONE remaining reachable leak was the legacy Action-Stripe: its 'ACTION STRIPE: ON' toggle still sat in the redesign SYS/overflow menu (#hdr-stripe-btn), and the stripe element (#action-stripe) is the legacy fixed-bottom rack toolbar. Added two body.rd-gated rules: body.rd #hdr-stripe-btn{display:none} hides the toggle from the default SYS menu, and body.rd #action-stripe{display:none!important} ensures the legacy stripe never renders in the default UI even if previously toggled-on/persisted or set contextually via stripeView_setRack. Per the directive, Action-Stripe is a LOW-confidence 'flag, don't cut' feature - this DEMOTES it (reversible, preserved intact under ?legacy=1), it does NOT cut it; Stage 6 deletion remains gated. CSS-only, no markup/JS/asset changes; legacy path untouched (byte-identical). Browser-verified: default UI - Action-Stripe toggle absent from SYS menu, stripe hidden, redesign rack detail still renders its own controls; ?legacy=1 - toggle back in menu, stripe restored. node --check x3 + sw.js, CSS brace gate, three-stamp lockstep. DEVICE-VERIFY: default UI SYS menu shows no ACTION STRIPE row and no legacy chrome anywhere; add ?legacy=1 and confirm the 5-tab app + ACTION STRIPE toggle fully return.
